title: Fix Date Control to Display Selected Date and Default to All Records
story: As a DataTools Pro user viewing reports, I want the date filter to default to showing all records and accurately display the selected date range, so that I start with a complete view and can filter down as needed.
description:
The date control on the Reports and Dashboards page has two issues:
1. **Display bug**: The date picker does not update to reflect the user's selection — the displayed dates stay the same regardless of what is picked. Possible JS library incompatibility.
2. **Default behavior**: The control should default to showing all records instead of a specific date range.

## Additional Issues Found
- Several dark mode CSS issues with the date picker were identified during testing
acceptance_criteria: Date picker displays the currently selected date range accurately
Date picker defaults to showing all records on page load
Relative date options (Last 7 days, Last 30 days, etc.) work correctly
Date filter correctly constrains the displayed reports
Date picker renders correctly in dark mode
ux_changes: Date picker now correctly displays the selected date range and defaults to showing all records. Dark mode styling fixed for the date picker.
implementation_notes: Fixed the date picker display sync issue — likely a JS library event binding problem. Default changed to 'all records'. Product owner open to alternative date picker libraries if the current one proves too unreliable. Relative date support (Last 7 days, etc.) is important and must be preserved.
comment_summary: Developer found additional dark mode issues during investigation. Team discussed defaulting to all records in a daily standup. Product owner expressed willingness to consider alternative date picker libraries if the current one becomes too problematic. Fix confirmed working on staging.
